metadata.name: q
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Quick-and-dirty debugging output for tired programmers

modules.name: q
documentation:
Quick and dirty debugging output for tired programmers.

All output goes to /tmp/q, which you can watch with this shell command:

    tail -f /tmp/q

If TMPDIR is set, the output goes to $TMPDIR/q.

To print the value of foo, insert this into your program:

    import q; q(foo)

To print the value of something in the middle of an expression, insert
"q()", "q/", or "q|".  For example, given this statement:

    file.write(prefix + (sep or '').join(items))

...you can print out various values without using any temporary variables:

    file.write(prefix + q(sep or '').join(items))  # prints (sep or '')
    file.write(q/prefix + (sep or '').join(items))  # prints prefix
    file.write(q|prefix + (sep or '').join(items))  # prints the arg to write

To trace a function's arguments and return value, insert this above the def:

    import q
    @q

To start an interactive console at any point in your code, call q.d():

    import q; q.d()
Copyright 2012 Google Inc.  All Rights Reserved.

classes.name: q.Q

methods.name: q.Q.unindent
documentation: Removes any indentation that is common to all of the given lines.

methods.name: q.Q.get_call_exprs
documentation: Gets the argument expressions from the source of a function call.

methods.name: q.Q.show
documentation: Prints out nice representations of the given values.

methods.name: q.Q.trace
documentation: Decorator to print out a function's arguments and return value.

methods.name: q.Q.__call__
documentation:
If invoked as a decorator on a function, adds tracing output to the
function; otherwise immediately prints out the arguments.

methods.name: q.Q.__truediv__
documentation: Prints out and returns the argument.

methods.name: q.Q.d
documentation: Launches an interactive console at the point where it's called.

class_variables.name: q.Q.__doc__
documentation: from the module's __doc__ above

class_variables.name: q.Q.__or__
documentation: a loose-binding operator

class_variables.name: q.Q.q
documentation: backward compatibility with @q.q

class_variables.name: q.Q.t
documentation: backward compatibility with @q.t

class_variables.name: q.Q.__name__
documentation: App Engine's import hook dies if this isn't present

inner_classes.name: q.Q.FileWriter
documentation: An object that appends to or overwrites a single file.

inner_classes.name: q.Q.Writer

methods.name: q.Q.Writer.write
documentation: Writes out a list of strings as a single timestamped unit.
Abstract away the output pipe, timestamping, and color support.

inner_classes.name: q.Q.Stanza

methods.name: q.Q.Stanza.add
documentation: Adds a list of strings that are to be printed on one line.
Abstract away indentation and line-wrapping.
When we insert Q() into sys.modules, all the globals become None, so we
have to keep everything we use inside the Q class.
